name: Triadic Drift-Diffusion Model of Eat/Restrain Decisions
description: A stochastic evidence-accumulation (drift-diffusion) model where a decision variable integrating reward drive, executive control, and valuation drifts toward an 'eat' or 'restrain' boundary over deliberation micro-steps. The drift rate is computed from the triadic brain-systems model (reward, executive control, valuation) plus homeostatic hunger. Executive control capacity depletes when food cues are present and recovers when absent, capturing the DLPFC hypoactivity and ego-depletion-adjacent dynamics described in the literature. The valuation signal is updated via reward learning that contrasts health-oriented vs. hedonic outcomes.

variables.name: decision_variable
variables.description: Accumulated evidence: positive favors eating, negative favors restraint. Reset each timestep.

variables.name: reward_drive
variables.description: Hedonic/reward activation from current food cue, derived from food palatability and proximity

variables.name: executive_control
variables.description: Current effective DLPFC-mediated cognitive control capacity; depletes near food, recovers away from food

variables.name: valuation_bias
variables.description: Net goal-congruent valuation signal: positive favors long-term health goals, negative favors immediate gratification. Updated via reward learning.

variables.name: hunger
variables.description: Homeostatic hunger signal; increases each step, decreases on eating

variables.name: drift_rate
variables.description: Computed drift rate: positive drifts toward eat boundary, negative toward restrain

parameters.name: reward_weight
parameters.source: Triadic model (ScienceDirect / Current Opinion in Behavioral Sciences, 2022)

parameters.name: control_weight
parameters.source: Set > alpha reflecting effective top-down regulation in healthy individuals (Springer, 2021)

parameters.name: control_depletion_rate
parameters.source: Per-timestep depletion under food cue exposure (ScienceDirect / Appetite, 2017)

parameters.name: control_recovery_rate
parameters.source: Per-timestep recovery when not near food cues (ScienceDirect / Appetite, 2017)

parameters.name: micro_steps
parameters.source: Number of DDM micro-steps per decision

parameters.name: high_palatability_threshold
parameters.source: Threshold to classify food as high-palatability for health reward computation

rules.description: Compute reward drive R from nearest food's palatability and distance
rules.pseudocode:
foods = perception.resources.food
if foods:
    nearest = min(foods, key=lambda f: abs(f.x - perception.x) + abs(f.y - perception.y))
    dist = abs(nearest.x - perception.x) + abs(nearest.y - perception.y)
    R = nearest.palatability / (1.0 + dist)
else:
    R = 0.0

rules.description: Compute drift rate nu from triadic competition: reward + hunger vs control, modulated by valuation
rules.pseudocode: nu = alpha * R + eta * H - beta * C + gamma * V

rules.description: Evidence accumulation via drift-diffusion with Gaussian noise over N_micro micro-steps
rules.pseudocode:
x = 0.0
decision = 'undecided'
for i in range(N_micro):
    noise = random.gauss(0, 1)
    x = x + nu * dt + sigma * sqrt(dt) * noise
    if x >= a_eat:
        decision = 'eat'
        break
    if x <= a_restrain:
        decision = 'restrain'
        break

rules.description: Executive control depletion near food cues and recovery when away
Valuation update based on health vs hedonic reward contrast after eating
rules.pseudocode:
if ate_last_step:
    r_hedonic = last_food_palatability
    r_health = 1.0 - last_food_palatability  # low-palatability food is healthier
    V = clip(V + lambda_V * (r_health - r_hedonic), -1.0, 1.0)
else:
    # small drift toward health goal when not eating
    V = clip(V + 0.01, -1.0, 1.0)

rules.description: Hunger increases each step, decreases substantially when agent eats

decision_logic.description: At each timestep, compute reward drive from food perception, run DDM evidence accumulation to decide eat vs restrain, then translate to grid action. Eat boundary reached → approach/eat food; restrain boundary reached → move away or stay; undecided → stay.
pseudocode: # 1. Update internal states from last step
ate_last_step = (last_action_result is not None) and last_action_result.get('consumed', False)
Update V using rule R5 (if ate, use last food's palatability)
Compute R using rule R1 from perception.resources.food
nu = alpha * R + eta * H - beta * C + gamma * V
x += nu * dt + sigma * sqrt(dt) * random.gauss(0,1)
nearest_food = find nearest food in perception.resources.food
if any food within distance 2: return Action(move_away_from_nearest_food)

perception_to_variables.food_sources: perception.resources.food  # list of {x, y, type, palatability}
perception_to_variables.food_distance: manhattan distance from (perception.x, perception.y) to each food (x, y)
perception_to_variables.ate_last_step: last_action_result.get('consumed', False) after an eat action

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action → ConsumeEffect reward (1.0); internal valuation uses palatability to compute health vs hedonic contrast

expected_behaviors.description: Executive control depletes when agent spends many steps near food, eventually crossing eat boundary even when previously restraining
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Place agent adjacent to high-palatability food for 20 steps with initial C=0.8 → assert C decreases and eventually decision shifts from 'restrain' to 'eat'

expected_behaviors.description: High hunger increases drift rate toward eating, making eat decisions faster/more likely
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Set H=0.9 with food nearby → run 10 decisions → assert majority are 'eat'; Set H=0.1 → assert majority are 'restrain'

expected_behaviors.description: Valuation bias shifts toward restraint after eating high-palatability food (hedonic > health reward)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Agent eats food with palatability=0.9 → assert V decreases (shifts negative); agent eats food with palatability=0.2 → assert V increases

expected_behaviors.description: Stochastic noise creates variability: same conditions produce different decisions across runs
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Fix all state variables, run DDM 100 times → assert both 'eat' and 'restrain' outcomes occur (not 100% one outcome)

expected_behaviors.description: Control recovery when agent moves away from food
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Deplete C to 0.3, then move agent far from food for 15 steps → assert C increases toward C_max

references: ScienceDirect / Current Opinion in Behavioral Sciences (2022) — The brain bases of regulation of eating behaviors
APA PsycNet (2023) — The brain bases of regulation of eating behaviors
CORE / Frontiers in Psychology (2016) — Impulsivity and Inhibitory Control Deficits Are Associated with Obesity
Springer / Reviews in Endocrine and Metabolic Disorders (2021) — Neurobiological regulation of eating behavior
ScienceDirect / Appetite (2017) — Executive functions and the self-regulation of eating behavior: A review
PMC (2017) — Executive functioning and dietary intake
